data.title: Kinetik GAAP EPS of $2.16, revenue of $430.4M
data.description:
• Kinetik press release (KNTK): Q4 GAAP EPS of $2.16.
  • Revenue of $430.4M.

  • Kinetik reported net income including noncontrolling interest of $416.7 million and $525.9 million for the three and twelve months ended December 31, 2025, respectively. Kinetik generated Adjusted EBITDA1 of $252.1 million and $987.7 million, Distributable Cash Flow1 of $151.7 million and $620.5 million, and Free Cash Flow1 of $(12.0) million and $167.2 million for the three and twelve months ended December 31, 2025, respectively.

data.title: Is Tesla now a buying opportunity? Analysts weigh in
data.description:
Should investors take advantage of the recent sell-off in Tesla (TSLA) shares or remain on the sidelines?

Recently published reports by Seeking Alpha and Wall Street analysts were a bit mixed on how good of a buy the stock was at current prices.

SA's The Asian Investor hiked their rating from sell to buy on Monday, asserting that  "bearish sentiment and a -22% YTD price return make shares appealing again from a fundamental point of view."

"TSLA is accelerating its shift to AI and robotics, winding down Model S/X production, and leveraging its robust, debt-free balance sheet for self-funded transformation," The Asian Investor wrote.

"Upcoming Q1 ’26 earnings and updates on autonomous driving and Optimus robotics could catalyze renewed investor optimism and multiplier re-rating," they added.

SA's Elizabeth Pramila was likewise bullish, raising her rating from a strong sell to a buy on April 11 to reflect "pivotal developments in autonomy and strategic direction." Pramila cited developments such as Cybercab mass production at the Giga Texas plant and imminent hardware upgrades that will "position TSLA at the forefront of autonomous vehicle innovation."

Pramila also asserted that despite the stock's decline, "TSLA's multiples are supported by a persistent 'Musk premium' and a 19% Street price target upside." 

SA analyst Dhierin Bechai was more cautious. 

"I have upgraded Tesla’s stock from Sell to Hold and that is on a 3-year median EV/EBITDA base, which is lower than the longer-term base, and on the condition that Tesla grows itself into the company that Elon Musk envisions. Until that time, it is a speculative opportunity," Bechai wrote in his analysis published over the weekend.

"I am currently assigning a Hold rating with a $449.52 price target, noting the various risks that Tesla’s transformation efforts bring in terms of execution risk and automotive pressures in the form of macro pressures and competition," Bechai added.

As for Wall Street, RBC Capital analysts on Monday maintained their outperform rating on Tesla, but lowered their price target to $480 from $500, citing concerns about Chinese competition and the scaling up of its robotics business, according to MarketWatch.

Tesla is expected to release its Q1 earnings report on April 22.

data.title: NRG Energy prices $2.6B in secured and unsecured notes offerings
data.description:
• NRG Energy (NRG) on Tuesday said it has priced $2.6 billion in senior secured and unsecured notes offerings.
  • The company said the issuance includes $500 million of 4.955% secured notes due 2031, $1.05 billion of 5.875% unsecured notes due 2034, and $1.05 billion of 6.125% unsecured notes due 2036.
  • NRG said it plans to use the proceeds, along with a proposed $900 million term loan, to repay existing debt, fund a tender offer for certain notes, and for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Commerzbank, Unicredit clash on value of Commerzbank, CEO says
data.description:
Commerzbank (CRZBF) (CRZBY) CEO Bettina Orlopp said talks with UniCredit SpA about a deal highlighted a gap between the two companies on their valuation of the German lender, according to a media report on Tuesday.

"For the time being, it is clear that our opinions differ quite a lot, starting with the valuation of the two companies and the exchange ratio," Orlopp said in a Bloomberg Television interview. "There are also some disagreements about the potential future business model."

Last month, UniCredit (UNCFF) (UNCRY) disclosed a €35B offer to increase the Italian bank's stake in Commerzbank to over 30%.

With the difference of opinion, Orlopp said the bank will maintain its standalone strategy, which the German government indicated it will support. 

Previously, Commerzbank (CRZBF) has said the hostile nature of the offer makes it “difficult” to build mutual trust to reach a deal.

UniCredit CEO Andrea Orcel has said the bid is designed to allow the bank to boost its stake in Commerzbank to over 30% without triggering a requirement to make an offer for complete ownership, Bloomberg said.

data.title: JD.com earns upgrade at Macquarie on Q1 outlook
data.description:
With advertising revenue seen offsetting soft electronics sales, JD.com (JD) is expected to report positive Q1 results on May 14, with revenue forecasted to grow 3% and operating margin to expand by 30 basis points.

This led Macquarie analysts Ellie Jiang and Jason Zhang to upgrade JD.com (JD) to Outperform from Neutral and set a $35 price target, which assumes 20% upside from Monday’s closing price.

The upgrade drove shares higher, leaving JD.com (JD) 8% higher at Tuesday’s close and above its 200-day moving average for the first time since October.

“We expect [JD Retail] to be largely on track, with the high base effect from the trade-in policies dragging on electronics’ sales, offset by faster-growing advertising revenue as [JD Retail]  expands into more third-party categories,” Jiang and Zhang wrote in their note to clients.

Wall Street analysts expect JD.com (JD) to have earned an adjusted profit of $0.50 per share on $45.56B in revenue in the first quarter. 

JD.com (JD) is viewed as a Strong Buy among Wall Street analysts, while Seeking Alpha authors and Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating view the stock as a Buy with a Quant score of 3.77 out of 5.

data.title: Rocket Lab officially enters Europe after closing on Mynaric deal
data.description:
Rocket Lab Corporation (RKLB) announced on Tuesday that the company closed on the $155.3M acquisition of Mynaric AG. The completion of the transaction comes after successful review and approval by Germany’s Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy.

Mynaric is a leading provider of laser optical communications terminals for air, space, and mobile applications. The deal included a nominal cash payment and 2,277,002 shares of Rocket Lab's (RKLB) stock. The integration of Mynaric is expected to further strengthen Rocket Lab's (RKLB) extensive capabilities as a leading launch provider, spacecraft manufacturer, and supplier of satellite components at scale to the global space market.

Mynaric will continue to be headquartered in Munich, Germany, establishing Rocket Lab's (RKLB) first European footprint and enabling the company to expand its ability to support German and broader European space programs.

Rocket Lab (RKLB) CEO Peter Beck noted Mynaric has a strong track record of unlocking satellite subsystem bottlenecks, making industry-leading technology affordable and available at scale.

data.title: Gloo Holdings, Inc. GAAP EPS of -$0.77 misses by $0.38, revenue of $33.6M beats by $1.58M
data.description:
• Gloo Holdings, Inc. press release (GLOO): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.77 misses by $0.38.
  • Revenue of $33.6M beats by $1.58M.

  • Raises fiscal year 2026 Revenue guidance to $190 million

    Expects more than 30% sequential improvement in Adjusted EBITDA from Q4 25 to Q1 26

data.title: Gloo to acquire Enterprisemarketdesk to expand Workday services
data.description:
• Gloo (Nasdaq: GLOO) on Tuesday said it has agreed to acquire Enterprisemarketdesk, a consulting and services partner for Workday.
  • Gloo said the acquisition will add AI-enabled Workday consulting, implementation and support services to its platform, targeting nonprofit and mid-market organizations.

  • The company said the deal is expected to close in the second quarter.

  • GLOO +21.89% after hours to $7.46.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Verizon Communications shares decline for the seventh consecutive session
data.description:
Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) shares fell 0.14% Tuesday to $45.36, extending the losing streak to seven sessions.

Over the preceding six sessions, the stock declined more than 8.06%, compared with a 4.61% gain in the S&P 500 Index. Despite the recent weakness, the shares are still up over 8.86% year to date, outperforming the index’s 0.60% rise.

Seeking Alpha analyst Bela Lakos maintained a Hold rating on VZ, citing attractive fundamentals supported by strong Q4 subscriber growth and a targeted $5B in annual cost savings.

However, the analyst flagged risks from rising debt levels and an uncertain interest rate environment, which could weigh on profitability. While the company’s free cash flow outlook and shareholder returns remain solid, the current valuation suggests limited upside, though income-focused investors may be drawn to its 5.6% dividend yield.

Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating also supports a Hold stance, with a score of 3.49 out of 5. The grading reflects a mixed profile for Verizon Communications, with an A+ for profitability indicating strong margins and earnings stability, a D for growth pointing to weak expansion, and a B+ for valuation proposing the stock is fairly priced with limited upside.

Separately, Wall Street and Seeking Alpha analysts have assigned Buy ratings to Verizon Communications. However, Quant scores of 3.76 and 3.54 indicate the ratings are approaching Hold territory, suggesting a more cautious stance on the stock.

data.title: S&P 500 ends more than 1% as Trump hints of 'imminent' talks with Iran
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages closed comfortably in the green on Tuesday as investors grew optimistic about potential U.S.-Iran peace talks that could ease tensions in the Middle East.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) finished +1.2%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) closed +2%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) ended +0.7%.

The gains followed comments from President Donald Trump indicating that Iran had reached out for potential negotiations, even as the U.S. moved forward with a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

Trump suggested that negotiations with Iran could take place “over the next two days” in Pakistan.

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 4 basis points lower at 4.25%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) dropped 3 basis points to 3.75%.

“Crude oil prices are falling, stocks and bonds are higher, and markets have calmed even though the U.S. has blockaded the Strait of Hormuz. The markets are optimistic that the end of hostilities could be on the horizon,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Andrew Hecht.

“However, risks remain high in the current environment. We can hope the market is correct, but we must remain vigilant when approaching any risk positions, with careful attention to risk-reward dynamics,” Hecht added.

On the economic front, the March U.S. Producer Price Index came in at +0.5% MoM vs. +1.2% consensus and +0.5% prior (revised from +0.7%), and +4.0% YoY vs. +4.7% consensus and +3.4% prior.

Core PPI, which excludes foods and energy, was +0.1% MoM vs. +0.5% consensus and +0.3% prior (revised from +0.5%), and +3.8% YoY vs. +4.2% consensus and +3.8% prior (revised from +3.9%).

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were lower at $92.17, while Brent (CO1:COM) was at $95.25 per barrel.
